info.name: TechieFinder API
info.description:
TechieFinder connects Nigerian users with local technicians. This collection covers every backend endpoint (see DOCUMENTATION.md's 'API Reference' section for the prose version).

## Quick start
1. Run the backend locally (`cd backend && mvn spring-boot:run`) -- default `baseUrl` points at `http://localhost:8080/api`.
2. Run **Auth > Register - Customer**, then **Auth > Register - Technician** -- these save tokens/ids into collection variables automatically via each request's Tests script.
3. Run **Technicians > Create Technician Profile**, then set up a service offering + location, then walk through **Bookings**, **Payments**, **Ratings**, **Messaging** in order.
4. Run **Auth > Login - Seeded Admin** to get an admin token (default dev credentials: admin@techiefinder.com / ChangeMe123!, overridable via ADMIN_EMAIL/ADMIN_PASSWORD) for the **Admin** folder.

Requests are ordered so running a folder top-to-bottom (Postman Runner or manually) chains correctly -- most later requests depend on variables saved by earlier ones.

request.description: Registers a USER account. Saves customerToken/customerUserId/customerEmail for later requests.
Registers a TECHNICIAN account. Saves technicianToken/technicianUserId/technicianEmail.
ADMIN accounts can never self-register -- this always returns 403. Admins are seeded or created by an existing admin only.

exec: pm.test('Self-registering as ADMIN is rejected (403)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));

request.description: Logs in as the default admin seeded by DataInitializer on first startup. Saves adminToken.

Redeems a refresh token for a new access/refresh pair without re-entering a password -- this is what the mobile app's Face ID/Touch ID quick-unlock calls under the hood. Run 'Register - Customer' or 'Login' first to populate customerRefreshToken.

Verifies a real Google ID token (JWKS-checked server-side) and finds-or-creates the matching user -- can't be exercised from Postman since a real token can't be fabricated here, included for the request shape. role is only used the first time this identity signs up; returns 400 if GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID isn't configured server-side.

Verifies a real Apple ID token and finds-or-creates the matching user -- can't be exercised from Postman since a real token can't be fabricated here, included for the request shape. firstName/lastName are only used the first time this identity signs up, since Apple never puts a name in the token itself; returns 400 if APPLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID/APPLE_OAUTH_BUNDLE_ID isn't configured server-side.
category query param is optional; omit it to get all available technicians.

Heuristic weighted-score ranking (rating, completion rate, proximity, category match with the caller's booking history, verification). Auth is optional -- personalizes further when a customer token is supplied; works fine for guests too if you remove the Authorization header.
Must be called once by a freshly-registered TECHNICIAN account before any other technician-only endpoint works. Saves technicianId.
Select an image file for the 'image' field before sending. Saves portfolioItemId.

formdata.value: Fixed a burst pipe under the sink

request.description: certificateFile is optional; select a PDF/image before sending if you want one attached. Saves certificationId.
technicianId is auto-filled from 'Create Technician Profile'.

body.raw:
{
  "technicianId": "{{technicianId}}",
  "scheduledDateTime": "2026-08-01T10:00:00",
  "serviceDescription": "Fix leaking kitchen pipe",
  "userNotes": "Please bring your own tools",
  "serviceAddress": "12 Allen Ave",
  "city": "Lagos",
  "state": "Lagos",
  "latitude": 6.6018,
  "longitude": 3.3515,
  "estimatedPrice": 15000
}

request.description: CONFIRMED/REJECTED/IN_PROGRESS/COMPLETED may only be set by the assigned technician.
Either the customer or the technician can cancel. Run this instead of Confirm/Start/Complete if you want to test the cancellation path.

Settles instantly against the wallet simulation by default. If PAYMENT_GATEWAY_PROVIDER names a real gateway with a real key, this instead returns a requiresRedirect + authorizationUrl for a real Paystack/Flutterwave checkout -- see DOCUMENTATION.md.

exec: console.log('Real gateway configured -- open authorizationUrl to complete checkout, then run "Verify Gateway Payment".');

request.description: Only meaningful when a real gateway is configured and the booking was paid via checkout redirect rather than the wallet. Finalizes a pending payment by asking the gateway for its status.

Public endpoint Paystack calls directly -- not meant to be called manually except to inspect the shape. The x-paystack-signature header is verified server-side (HMAC-SHA512 of the raw body using the Paystack secret key); a wrong signature returns 403.
Public endpoint Flutterwave calls directly. verif-hash must match FLUTTERWAVE_WEBHOOK_SECRET_HASH exactly (a static shared secret, not an HMAC).
Only once per COMPLETED booking -- run 'Complete Job' first. Saves ratingId.

item.name: Get or Create Conversation with Technician

request.description: Idempotent -- returns the existing conversation if one already exists. Saves conversationId.
Saves the first notification's id for 'Mark Notification Read'.
Suspended accounts are rejected at login with a 401. Admin accounts can't be suspended (this always 403s if targeted at an admin's own id).
status: PENDING | VERIFIED | REJECTED | SUSPENDED
Removes a review outright and recalculates the technician's average rating.
status: PENDING | VERIFIED | REJECTED
